theme_and_archetype.description: Tactical Field Report / Humanitarian Command Center. A blend of rigid, high-contrast structural elements for clarity, warmed up by organic neutral base colors (bone white/sage) to maintain humanity. No generic tech-SaaS blues or AI purple gradients.
theme_and_archetype.emotional_tone: Urgent, transparent, authoritative, yet compassionate and grounded.

rules.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-black tracking-tighter leading-none text-left
rules.labels_overlines: font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.2em] font-medium text-secondary

typography.anti_patterns: DO NOT use Inter, Space Grotesk, Roboto. Avoid centered text for long paragraphs or dense data.

spacing_and_layout.padding_philosophy: Generous spacing. p-6 or p-8 inside bordered containers.
spacing_and_layout.alignment: Strictly left-aligned optical alignment. Asymmetrical hierarchy where main map or priority feed spans 8 columns, while secondary stats span 4 columns.

borders_and_shadows.shadows: Avoid soft drop shadows. Use hard 1px borders (border-collapse look) or solid offset shadows (4px 4px 0px rgba(17,18,19,1)) for interactive elements.
borders_and_shadows.surface_strategy: Grid Borders: Expose the skeleton. Data-heavy sections should have visible borders to mimic tactical printed reports.

shadcn_overrides.card: Remove default shadow. Use 1px solid border (border-borders-default), rounded-sm, p-6. For urgent cards, apply a 2px left border using status colors.
shadcn_overrides.button: Primary: Solid #E63946, zero radius, uppercase font-mono tracking-wide. Secondary: Outline with transparent bg, text-primary, border-primary.
shadcn_overrides.badge: Sharp corners, font-mono text-xs. Map to status colors. Never use generic pastel pills.
shadcn_overrides.table: Dense padding (py-2 px-3), mono font for numerical columns, left aligned headers.
shadcn_overrides.tabs: Underline style only, no pill backgrounds. Bold text for active tab.

specific_ui_elements.live_map: Leaflet container spanning full height/width of its grid area. Apply CSS filter to tiles (e.g., grayscale, high contrast) or use CartoDB Positron for a stark, tactical look. Heatmaps should use red/orange/yellow spectrum without smooth blurring—keep data points slightly pixelated/clustered.
specific_ui_elements.prioritization_feed: List UI with strict hierarchy. Right-side column for AI scores (using JetBrains Mono), left-side for descriptive text.
specific_ui_elements.forms: Left-aligned labels. Input fields with sharp 1px borders, focus state expands border to 2px black. No placeholder text, use explicit labels.

motion_and_interaction.hover_states: Hard background color flips. e.g., Hovering a secondary card changes background to #111213 and text to #F6F5F2.
motion_and_interaction.transitions: Fast and snappy. transition-all duration-150 ease-out.
motion_and_interaction.loading: Use mono text 'GATHERING INTEL...' or an ASCII/Grid scanning effect instead of standard spinners. No bouncy dots.

media_and_assets.icons: Phosphor Icons (Duotone or Bold weight) to provide a solid, legible aesthetic on dense dashboards.
media_and_assets.image_style: Documentary, high-contrast photography. Desaturated backgrounds with vibrant focal points (like orange vests).

hero_background.description: Folded paper contour map texture to be used as a subtle, low-opacity background for the login or hero section.

gallery_relief_1.description: Proof of completion / Volunteer operations (Boxes of supplies)

drone_view_disaster.description: Drone shot of rescue operations to be used in disaster mode toggles or emergency banners.

volunteer_portrait.description: Leaderboard avatar or volunteer profile shot.

testing_requirements.attributes: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="submit-incident-button"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure `react-leaflet` and `leaflet` are installed for mapping.
Implement the 'Control Room' grid approach: do not leave empty whitespace between cards, use consistent gap-4 or gap-6 and force columns to fill available height.
For the disaster mode toggle, swap the entire palette to a dark mode with #E63946 (Red) borders and stark white text to signal critical urgency.
Do NOT use transparent navigation headers. Ensure all toolbars and navigation elements have a solid background.
Adhere to the typography rules: Chivo for headings, Manrope for body, JetBrains Mono for all numerical data, scores, and timestamps.
